tips.metrics_good: Your bullets carry real numbers. Keep that quantified voice across the whole resume.

tips.strong_verbs: Strong verbs throughout — no "responsible for" or "helped with" anywhere. Keep it.
tips.no_bullets: This resume has no bullet points yet. Break experience into outcome-led bullets.
tips.too_many_bullets: A lot of bullets here. Trim to the 3–5 strongest per role so the best work stands out.
tips.good_density: Good density. Click ✦ on any bullet to sharpen it, or Tailor to target a specific job.

meta.title: AI Job Application Agent & AI Mock Interviews | RoboApply
meta.description: RoboApply is an AI job-search agent: it finds jobs that fit, scores them against your resume, drafts citation-checked cover letters for your approval — then a live AI interviewer preps you to win the offer. Free to start. 9 languages.
meta.ogDescription: Your AI agent matches jobs to your real resume, drafts applications you approve before anything sends, and preps you with live AI mock interviews in 9 languages. Start free.
meta.keywords: AI job application agent, auto apply to jobs, AI applies to jobs for you, AI mock interview practice, AI interview practice, AI cover letter generator, AI resume builder, job search automation

hero.eyebrow: Your AI job-search agent · works while you sleep
hero.subheadline: It scouts real fits overnight, scores them against your actual resume, and drafts cover letters that cite your real experience — then stages everything for your approval.
hero.reassure: Free plan · No card required · Autopilot in early access

log.aria: Sample overnight activity log: the agent scans live job boards at 23:04, matches openings against your resume and intent, drafts citation-checked cover letters through the night, stages twelve applications, opens your review window at 7:00 where you veto two and approve ten, sends the approved ten at 9:00, and emails a morning digest at 9:02 pointing you to interview practice.
log.title: roboapply — overnight.log

l2.msg: 41 fit your intent · scored against your resume

l3.msg: cover letter 03/12 · every claim cited to your resume

l8.msg: 10 applications out · your name, your words

l9.msg: morning report sent · now go practice the interview

hero.strip_summary: 23:00 → 09:00 · 12 drafted · 10 approved by you · 0 sent without you

problem.title: The volume game is rigged.
problem.sub: AI flooded hiring. In one Business Insider test, a mass-apply bot got a 6% response rate — recruiters can tell — while human-reviewed, human-approved applications consistently do better. The fix isn't more volume. It's a better agent, with your judgment in the loop.

s1.label: applicants now compete for the average corporate opening

s2.label: more applications flooding recruiters since AI mass-apply took off

s3.label: of job seekers believe under a quarter of their applications are ever read by a human

problem.source: Sources: The Interview Guys (242) · Greenhouse via Fortune (+400%) · candidate surveys, 2025–2026 (41%)

how.title: One night, start to finish.
how.sub: Set your intent once. The agent works the hours you shouldn't — and hands you the controls at the moments that matter.

s1.title: It hunts while you sleep
s1.body: The agent sweeps live job boards and filters hard against your stated intent — role, level, stack, location, salary. Wrong-fit postings are rejected before you ever see them.

s2.title: It writes with receipts
s2.body: A tailored resume and a real cover letter for every match — and every claim in the letter must cite a line from your actual history. Invented experience can't get through: the citation check is enforced in code.

s3.title: You hold the veto
s3.body: Everything waits in a review window. Skim the queue over coffee, open any draft, skip anything that feels off. Nothing is ever sent in your name without your OK.

s4.title: Out by 9:00 — then the real prep starts
s4.body: Approved applications go out at the start of a recruiter's day, under your name and your words — one tap from you, or hands-free as autopilot early access rolls out. Then the digest hits your inbox, and the interview studio is one tap away.

loop.title: One agent. The whole search.
loop.sub: Everything between “I need a job” and “I signed” lives in one place — and the same agent carries your context through all of it.

match.title: Matching that reads your resume
match.body: Every job gets an AI match score with written reasons, computed against your actual resume — not keyword bingo. Scores refresh when your resume changes.

resume.title: A resume that rewrites itself
resume.body: Import from LinkedIn or PDF, let AI rewrite weak bullets, tailor to any job description with a reviewable diff — then export a clean PDF or DOCX.

apply.title: Applications with a consent layer
apply.body: Citation-checked cover letters, a staging queue, a review hold you can veto, hard daily caps. Full hands-off autopilot is rolling out in early access — the consent layer shipped first, on purpose.

studio.title: An interviewer to practice against
studio.body: A live voice AI interviewer with 18 domain playbooks and 28 formats — phone screens to system design to case interviews — that grades you the way the real panel will.

studio.sub: 63% of job seekers have already faced an AI interviewer in real hiring (Greenhouse, 2026). Rehearse against ours — unlimited, judgment-free reps until the real one feels familiar.

f1.title: It talks — and interrupts — like a person
f1.body: Full-duplex live voice: the interviewer probes, pushes back, and follows up in real time, in 9 languages with native-accent voices.

f2.title: 18 domain playbooks, not trivia decks
f2.body: Law, finance, healthcare, hardware, AI/ML and more — it asks what insiders actually ask, and grades through the same lens.

f3.title: It does homework before you meet
f3.body: The interviewer researches current market requirements for your exact role and job description, then builds a custom interview plan around your resume.

f4.title: A report that changes your next answer
f4.body: Dimension scores, per-question corrections with stronger suggested answers, a full transcript, and a replayable recording after every session.

f5.title: A coach in your ear — if you want one
f5.body: Optional live nudges when you ramble, dodge the question, or bury the lede. Camera-on mode adds real video-call pressure.

report.quote: “Lead with the metric — you buried the 40% cost reduction at the end of your answer.”

rules.title: Hard-coded, not fine print.
rules.sub: These aren't settings buried in a menu. They're how the agent is built — so what lands in a recruiter's inbox never reads like bot spam.

items.r1_body: every claim in every cover letter must cite your actual resume — invented experience is blocked in code.
items.r2_body: nothing submits without a preview window you can veto, application by application.
items.r3_body: jobs that miss your stated targets — level, stack, location, salary — never reach your queue.
items.r4_body: daily volume is hard-capped; every application is deliberate, so your name stays credible.
items.r5_body: pause the agent in one click — and account deletion is a real wipe, not a soft-delete.

pricing.title: Start free. Upgrade when it's working.
pricing.sub: Every plan includes the whole agent — resume AI, job matching, the tracker, and the consent layer. Paid plans buy more interview practice.
pricing.credit_note: 1 credit = one 20-minute mock interview, billed by the second. Credits refill monthly. Pay by card — or Alipay in China.

free.name: Free
free.note: The full toolkit, plus one graded mock interview every month.

starter.name: Starter
starter.note: Serious-search cadence: practice before every real interview.

growth.name: Growth
growth.note: All-in: daily reps until the offer is signed.

q1.a: RoboApply is an AI job-search agent. It finds openings that match your intent, scores them against your actual resume, tailors your documents, drafts citation-checked cover letters, and stages every application for your approval — then a live AI interviewer helps you practice for the interviews that come back.

q2.q: Will RoboApply apply to jobs without my permission?
q2.a: No. Consent is the architecture, not a setting: applications only ever go out after your explicit approval, daily volume is hard-capped, and hands-free autopilot is opt-in early access. Nothing is submitted in your name without your OK.

q3.q: Do AI-written cover letters get flagged by recruiters?
q3.a: Generic AI letters do. RoboApply's letters are different by construction: every claim must cite a line from your real resume — the citation check is enforced in code, so invented experience never ships. And you review and approve each letter before it goes anywhere.

q4.q: Can I really practice a job interview with an AI?
q4.a: Yes — with a live voice interviewer that talks, probes, and interrupts like a person. Choose from 28 formats and 18 domain playbooks (software, law, finance, healthcare and more), interview in any of 9 languages, and get a graded report with per-question corrections, a transcript, and a recording.

q5.q: How is RoboApply different from auto-apply bots like LazyApply?
q5.a: Volume bots mass-submit without judgment — in one Business Insider test, a mass-apply bot got a 6% response rate, and recruiters can tell. RoboApply keeps your judgment in the loop with a review hold, citation-checked letters, and hard caps, and it closes the loop nobody else does: the same agent that applies also preps you for the interview.

q6.a: Free forever for the core toolkit plus one mock interview a month. Starter is $15/month with 10 practice interviews; Growth is $29/month with 28. A credit is a 20-minute session billed by the second, refilled monthly. Pay by card, or Alipay in China.

q7.a: Nine: English, Spanish, Portuguese, French, German, Japanese, Korean, and Simplified and Traditional Chinese. Practice interviews work in all nine today — the interviewer's voice and your graded report. The interface is fully localized in English, Japanese, and both Chinese variants, with the remaining languages rolling out now.

q8.q: Is my data safe — and can I actually delete it?
q8.a: Your resume, transcripts, and recordings are yours; nothing is shared with employers. Every AI action is logged to a transparent usage ledger, and account deletion is a real one-click wipe that purges your data — not a soft-delete that keeps it around.

final.sub: Three minutes of setup: resume in, intent set, agent briefed. And the interview studio is open right now — run your first practice session free.

footer.note: © RoboApply. Built by candidates, for candidates.

agent.sub: Most people start in the middle and dial up after the first 5 applications come back clean.
agent.mode_manual_desc: I draft. You review every send. Zero auto-applies.
agent.mode_balanced_desc: I auto-apply to ≥ 90 matches. You review the rest.
agent.mode_aggressive_desc: Every match ≥ 80 ships. I flag anything risky first.

agent.quiet_sub: I'll work but won't time-stamp anything in this window
agent.auto_decline_sub: If the role is outside your filter, I send a polite no on your behalf
agent.auto_schedule_sub: When a recruiter sends a calendar link, I pick the best slot from your free time
agent.pause_interviews_sub: Don't spread yourself thin — let the current pipeline play out
agent.rescore_sub: As your resume evolves, I re-rank stale matches to your current profile
agent.coach_sub: How chatty your AI co-pilot should be

notif.sub: By default I'm quiet. Turn things on as you trust the agent more.
notif.event_queueReview_sub: An auto-apply is about to send
notif.digest_sub: A roll-up of what I did, no individual pings

integ.sub: The more I'm connected, the less you need to babysit. I can fetch responses from Gmail, schedule on your Calendar, and ping you in Slack.

privacy.sub: Stealth mode is on by default. Your current employer never sees you. Recruiters at companies you've blocked never reach you.
privacy.visibility_sub: Who can find you in inbound searches
privacy.blocked_sub: These never see your profile or applications
privacy.export_sub: All applications, drafts, transcripts, and resumes as JSON + PDF
privacy.retention_sub: How long I keep transcripts of interviews and drafts

plan.sub: Cancel anytime. Pause anytime. The hunt doesn't stop just because you took a vacation.
plan.premium_b2: Concierge: a real human reviews top 3 drafts/week

danger.sub: These don't ask twice — well, actually they ask twice. But you should still be sure.
danger.pause_desc: I'll stop scanning, applying, and replying. Your data stays. You can resume any time.
danger.reset_desc: Wipes all settings on this page. Resumes and applications remain.
danger.delete_data_desc: Clears your match history, queue, activity log, and pipeline. Account stays.
danger.delete_data_confirm: This clears your match history, queue, activity log, and pipeline. Your account and resumes stay.
danger.delete_data_error: Couldn't clear your data. Please try again.
danger.delete_data_success: Done — cleared {count} records. Your account and resumes are untouched.
danger.delete_account_desc: Wipes everything. Resumes, applications, transcripts, integrations. Can't be undone.

errors.error_body: We've logged the issue. You can try again or head back home.

queue.eyebrow: {count, plural, one {# pending review} other {# pending review}}
queue.title: Read these before
queue.sub: I'll auto-submit each one when its timer runs out. Edit the cover, skip it, or fire now — you're the boss.
queue.actionError: That didn't go through. Give it another try.

countdown.title: I'll submit this automatically when the timer runs out — unless you act first.

empty.title: Queue
empty.sub: New matches land here when they cross your threshold. I'll draft each one and hold it for your review before sending.

error.title: Couldn't load the queue
error.body: Something hiccuped on my end. Your drafts are safe — try again.

editCover.title: Edit cover letter
editCover.subtitle: For {title} at {company}
editCover.hint: Markdown supported. I'll send this exact text.
editCover.tooLong: Too long — keep it under {max} characters.

today.noReasoning: I don't have a detailed read on this one yet.
today.appliedBanner: Sent. Cover, tailored resume, and screening answers submitted.
today.passedBanner: Removed from your feed. I'll learn from this and skip similar roles.

sub.direct: {scanned} jobs scanned, {matched} cleared your threshold, {autoApplied} auto-applied. The strongest are queued — your call.
sub.playful: Brewed coffee while you slept. {scanned} listings scanned, {matched} matched, {autoApplied} auto-applied. {queued} more are waiting for your green light.
sub.formal: {scanned} listings reviewed overnight. {matched} met the match threshold. {autoApplied} applications submitted; {queued} awaiting your approval.

sub_noqueue.direct: {scanned} jobs scanned, {matched} cleared your threshold, {autoApplied} auto-applied.
sub_noqueue.playful: Brewed coffee while you slept. {scanned} listings scanned, {matched} matched, {autoApplied} auto-applied.
sub_noqueue.formal: {scanned} listings reviewed overnight. {matched} met the match threshold. {autoApplied} applications submitted.

empty.title: All caught up,
empty.sub: No fresh matches above your threshold right now. I'll keep scanning and drop new ones here the moment they land.

error.title: Couldn't load your
error.sub: Something hiccuped on my end. Your feed is safe — try again.

activity.title: Everything
activity.sub: Receipts for every application, draft, decline, and quiet decision. No black box.

empty.title: Nothing logged
empty.sub: Once I start applying, tailoring, and triaging on your behalf, every action shows up here — with my reasoning.

error.title: Couldn't load your activity
error.body: Something hiccuped on my end. Your history is safe — try again.

sub.direct: Synced automatically from inbox replies and calendar invites. Drag a card — or use its status menu — to move it across the {columns} stages.
sub.playful: I keep this in sync from your inbox and calendar. Drag a card to nudge it along, or pick a new stage from its menu.
sub.formal: Stages update automatically from inbox replies and calendar invites. Reassign a card by dragging it or via its status control.

card.drag_hint: Draggable card — drag to another stage or use the status menu

empty.saved: Nothing saved yet — bookmark a role and it'll wait here.
empty.offer: No offers yet — I'll celebrate when one lands ✨

empty_board.title: Your pipeline is
empty_board.body: Once you save or apply to roles, they'll show up here so you can track every conversation in one place.

error.title: Couldn't load your pipeline
error.body: Something hiccuped on my end. Your data is safe — try again.

setup.title: Practice. Until
setup.sub: Pick a role, pick an interviewer, pick a vibe. I'll run the gauntlet. Your coach watches from your side and feeds you nudges live.
setup.startError: Couldn't start that session. Give it another try.
setup.insufficientCredits: This interview needs {required} credits but you have {balance}. Top up to continue.

recent.label: Pick up where you left off

role.title: Pick a role
role.jdPlaceholder: Paste the full job description — title, responsibilities, requirements. I'll rewrite it into an interview brief.
role.jdHint: Paste the full JD — I'll rewrite it into a structured interview brief and ground the questions in it.
role.jdTooShort: Add a bit more — paste the responsibilities and requirements so the brief is accurate.

interviewer.title: Pick your interviewer
interviewer.sub: Distinct interviewer styles — pick the challenge you want to practice.

type.title: Pick the format
type.sub: Run one or chain a full loop

format.title: How you'll show up
format.sub: Video gets you ready for the real thing. Voice is fine for warm-ups.

video.title: Video call
video.desc: Camera on, face-to-face. I'll score your eye contact, presence, and how you handle the silence.

voice.title: Voice only
voice.desc: Audio only — like a phone screen. Good for working out structure before turning the camera on.

langDuration.title: Language & duration
langDuration.sub: Pick the language I'll interview you in and how long we'll run.

error.title: Couldn't load the interview gym
error.body: Something hiccuped on my end. Try again in a moment.

preview.title: Market job requirements
preview.sub: What this interview will screen for — grounded in live job-board postings.
preview.empty: Pick a role or paste a JD, choose an interviewer and a type, then preview the requirements driving your questions.
preview.error: Preview unavailable — your interview will still be tailored to this role.
preview.sourcesGeneric: Based on a market profile for this role.

maya.blurb: Conversational and kind. Draws out your story with gentle follow-ups. No tricks, no pressure.
maya.style: Calm, unhurried pace · open-ended questions · reflective listening · encouraging · never adversarial
maya.company: Talent Partner at a high-growth scale-up

june.blurb: Mission-obsessed and personable. Wants to know if you genuinely care — why this, why now.
june.style: Friendly and energetic · values- and motivation-first · 'why this company?' · warm but tests sincerity once
june.company: Founder & CEO of an early-stage startup

nova.blurb: Curious and fast-moving. Hops across the whole landscape — 'what else is out there?'
nova.style: Wide-ranging · compare-the-alternatives · trend-aware · pivots sideways, never drills
nova.company: Principal at a global consulting firm

priya.blurb: STAR-method enforcer. 'Tell me about a time…' until your story has a real action and result.
priya.style: Story-based · STAR enforcement · patient follow-ups · conflict & failure scenarios · separates 'we' from 'you'
priya.company: People Lead at a large consumer brand

rex.blurb: Hands you a problem you've never seen and watches how you break it down — not the answer.
rex.style: Lateral thinking · estimation & hypotheticals · reason-out-loud · changes one constraint mid-answer

diaz.blurb: Calm working-manager who's done the job. Keeps asking 'what did YOU specifically do?'
diaz.style: Even-keeled · practice-over-theory · 'one real example, step by step' · separates doers from describers

atlas.blurb: Senior generalist who's seen every corner of the field. Maps your coverage, probes the gaps.
atlas.style: Breadth-first · cross-domain · landscape-mapping · exposes blank spots by jumping to the next area
atlas.company: Distinguished Engineer at a major tech firm

bishop.blurb: Wants the failure story, the conflict you lost, and proof you owned it — not the highlight reel.
bishop.style: Leadership-principles · ownership-obsessed · demands failure & conflict · distrusts rehearsed answers · 'what did YOU do?'
bishop.company: Hiring Director at a high-growth scale-up

okonkwo.blurb: Rigorous case interviewer who keeps moving the goalposts. Cares only how you think under pressure.
okonkwo.style: First-principles framing · structured decomposition · escalating constraints · probes assumptions · composure under ambiguity
okonkwo.company: Partner at a top-tier strategy firm

kai.blurb: Loves edge cases, digs three levels down. Wants the exact decision and the case where it broke.
kai.style: Technical depth · quiet pauses · 'walk me through exactly what happened' · relentless on the mechanism
kai.company: Senior Staff Engineer on a large-scale infra team

voss.blurb: Pushes back on every claim. Demands the metric behind the story, not the vibe.
voss.style: Pointed · skeptical · numbers-first · 'how do you know that?' · accepts only evidence-backed depth

lena.blurb: Cares how clearly you land the point. Will ask you to explain the hard thing simply.
lena.style: Audience-first · 'explain it like I'm new' · rewards a clean headline · catches jargon and rambling
lena.company: Comms lead who's coached hundreds of execs

mirae.blurb: Jumps topics every two minutes and keeps the clock running. Can you stay sharp and adapt?
mirae.style: Fast topic-switching · time-boxed answers · high tempo · rewards adaptability and poise
mirae.company: Final-round panel chair at a big-tech firm

osei.blurb: Looks for how you treat people when it's hard. Asks for the time you put the team over the win.
osei.style: Values-in-action · collaboration and inclusion stories · 'how did the team feel?' · warm but probing
osei.company: Head of People at a values-driven company

devi.blurb: Hands you an open problem and works it WITH you. Cares how you frame and reason, not the answer.
devi.style: Collaborative reasoning · 'let's figure it out together' · nudges your framing · thinking over the right answer
devi.company: Product strategist who reasons out loud with you

amara.blurb: Sweeps across markets and disciplines, hunting for range and the connections you can draw.
amara.style: Cross-industry · maps the whole market · 'who else does this well?' · connects ideas across domains
amara.company: Growth advisor across dozens of industries

sterling.blurb: Senior, exacting, unhurried. Wants the executive summary first — then the proof, crisply.
sterling.style: Formal and polished · headline-first · concise under scrutiny · tests poise and precision of language

tariq.blurb: Pushes hard and fast, talks over you, flips your answer back. Watching whether you hold steady.
tariq.style: Rapid-fire · interrupts and challenges · plays devil's advocate · tests composure, not just answers

screening.label: Phone Screen
screening.sub: Background, fit, motivation, logistics — the gate before the loop

behavioral.label: Behavioral (STAR)
behavioral.sub: Real past stories: ownership, conflict, failure, influence, leadership

technical.label: Live Coding / Technical
technical.sub: Real-time problem solving in a shared editor: data structures, algorithms, correctness

system.label: System / Architecture Design
system.sub: Design a large-scale system end to end; defend scale & trade-offs

case.label: Case / Business Problem
case.sub: Open business problem: structure, data, recommendation

culture.label: Values / Culture Fit
culture.sub: Values alignment, motivation, working style, integrity under ambiguity

panel.label: Panel / Mixed Loop
panel.sub: Rapid-fire mix across competencies, as a multi-interviewer panel would

take_home.label: Take-Home Defense
take_home.sub: Defend & extend a self-paced project: design choices, trade-offs, scaling

debugging.label: Debugging / Code Review
debugging.sub: Diagnose a broken program or critique existing code/PR

incident_sre.label: Incident / SRE Scenario
incident_sre.sub: Triage a live production incident: stabilize, command-line, post-mortem

sql_analytics.label: SQL / Analytics Exercise
sql_analytics.sub: Hands-on queries + metric reasoning against real tables

product_sense.label: Product Sense / Execution
product_sense.sub: Open product design prompt + metrics: structure, users, prioritize, measure

portfolio.label: Portfolio / Past-Work Review
portfolio.sub: Walk through real past work: process, decisions, contribution, impact

design_critique.label: Design Critique / Whiteboard
design_critique.sub: Critique an app or design live, or sketch a solution to a fresh prompt

research_design.label: UX Research Study Design
research_design.sub: Design a study on the spot or critique a flawed research plan

sales_roleplay.label: Mock Sales Call / Role-Play
sales_roleplay.sub: Live discovery, cold call, or escalation against a role-played counterpart

pitch_demo.label: Pitch / Demo Presentation
pitch_demo.sub: Deliver a tailored demo or pitch to a stakeholder panel, then field pushback

account_strategy.label: Account / Success Case
account_strategy.sub: Analyze an account and build an onboarding/retention/expansion plan

modeling_test.label: Financial Modeling / Excel
modeling_test.sub: Build or stress an LBO / 3-statement / DCF / FP&A model and defend it

finance_technical.label: Finance Technical Q&A
finance_technical.sub: Accounting, valuation, deal mechanics, and an investment thesis

clinical_scenario.label: Clinical Scenario
clinical_scenario.sub: Reason through a realistic patient/unit situation: assess, prioritize, escalate

situational_judgement.label: Situational Judgement (SJT)
situational_judgement.sub: Ranked/spoken workplace dilemmas testing ethics, professionalism, judgment

practical_skills.label: Practical Skills / Trade Test
practical_skills.sub: Narrated hands-on task: technique, safety, code compliance, diagnosis

teaching_demo.label: Teaching Demo
teaching_demo.sub: Deliver a short lesson, engage learners, then defend your approach

ops_case.label: Operations / Process Case
ops_case.sub: Diagnose a process or supply-chain disruption; prioritize improvements

presentation.label: Presentation Interview
presentation.sub: Prepare and deliver a structured presentation, then field challenge

leadership_strategy.label: Leadership / Strategy
leadership_strategy.sub: Team-building, vision, and a 90-day plan for leadership & exec roles

reference_check.label: Reference / Credentialing
reference_check.sub: Verification-stage probing of track record, reliability, and credentials

live.waitForQuestion: Wait for the question to finish
live.composerPlaceholder: Speak it, type it, or do both. ⌘+Enter to send.
live.transcriptEmpty: Transcript appears here as you both talk…

expired.title: This session expired
expired.body: I couldn't find that interview — it may have already ended or the link is stale. Start a fresh one.

live.transcriptEmpty: The transcript will appear here as you talk.

live.connectionLostBody: Your interview is still open — rejoin to pick up where you left off, or end it now to get your report.
live.micDeniedBody: We couldn't access your microphone, so the interviewer wouldn't be able to hear you. Allow microphone access in your browser, then try again.
live.deviceFailure: There's a problem with your microphone or camera. Check your device settings — the interviewer may not hear or see you.
live.interviewerUnavailableBody: We couldn't bring the AI interviewer into the room. This is usually temporary — try again in a moment.

coach.noHint: Just answer naturally — you've got this.
coach.coachModeHint: Live hints and nudges while you answer. Turn off for a real-feel run.

live.autoRejoinBody: We're bringing you back into your interview automatically. Hang tight — if this doesn't work you'll be able to rejoin manually.

report.title: Interview report
report.processing: Finalizing your interview — the recording and score will appear shortly.
report.analysisPending: Your detailed analysis is being generated — this can take up to a minute.

cta.alipaySwitchNote: To change plans, cancel and re-subscribe at checkout.

credits.title: How credits work
credits.explainer: 1 credit = one 20-minute mock interview. Shorter or longer sessions are charged proportionally, and your credits refill at the start of each billing month.

faq.title: Questions, answered
faq.a1: One credit gives you a 20-minute AI mock interview with a full report afterward. Use them whenever you want to practice.
faq.q2: What happens to credits I don't use?
faq.a2: Each plan refills your credits at the start of every billing month. Unused credits don't roll over, so practice often!
faq.q3: Can I change or cancel my plan?
faq.a3: Yes — upgrade, downgrade, or cancel anytime from your account. Changes take effect at the end of your current billing month.
faq.q4: Which payment methods can I use?
faq.a4: We accept major cards via Stripe internationally, and Alipay for users in China. Your region is detected automatically — switch it with the toggle above.
faq.a5: Yes. Your recordings and reports are yours alone and are never shared with employers or other users.

checkout.failed: We couldn't start checkout. Please try again, or switch your payment region above.
